paragraphs.text: The observation is that digital platforms accumulate both personal and state sensitive data. They seek to have full control over transfers of information and data. Both substance (ideologies) and form (proprietary formats, privatisation of networks, centralisation and data transfer filtering). The European states MUST react quickly and intensively. Europe that needs to react as well. All sensitive data such as personal information, health information, etc. must be managed by sovereign entities (Member States and/or European Union) which have a duty and responsibility towards their citizens.  The private interests and the interests of the citizens have not been and will be in agreement! States such as the European Union must stop entrusting sovereign digital services (tax, search, armed, personal data, etc.) to private companies. States, like the European Union, must immediately regain control of the digital world, which is NOT a disconnected world, but rather an EXTENSION of the REEL world, and must therefore comply with the same rules, laws and governing. For the most stable security and serenity possible, Europe and the Member States must allocate a substantial budget to the establishment and maintenance of a SOUVERAIN digital network. Given that digital technology has evolved so far and as digital companies have pushed their model, believing that a public/private partnership would benefit citizens and the interests of nations and the European Union would be pure. It is clear that the most important players in modern digital have only to do laws, decency, or any interest other than their finances. Thank you for taking responsibility, looking at reality in the face of it, and acting accordingly.
